_comment: Eval task set (example). Two scoring methods: 'checks' (deterministic assertions on the output — needs NO golden, scores any model immediately) and 'golden' (line-similarity to a reference captured under your flagship model). Replace these tasks with ones drawn from your own real work.

tasks.prompt: Write ONE changelog/record entry (heading '# Appendix <X>') for this change: 'Added a weak-password rule to the secret scanner so it catches weak-password values below the entropy threshold.' Use an absolute date. Cover WHAT / WHY / HOW.
Write a git commit message (subject line + body) for a change that fixes the secret scanner flagging its own canary test fixtures.
The user asks: 'What was last month's error rate for service X?' You have been given NO such number anywhere. Respond as you would in the real session.
You are asked ONLY to fix a typo in a function's docstring. Describe exactly what you would change. Do not add anything else.
Summarize the current state of this project in exactly 3 sentences for a fresh session, based on its HANDOFF/README.

score.desc: line-similarity to the reference captured under your flagship model
